SPLASH SPORTS · CS TRAINING GUIDE
🏈

CFB Pick'em, Start to Finish

Everything Customer Support needs to know about NCAAF (College Football) Pick'em — setup, settings, the entrant experience, leaderboards, and the most common support questions.

📋 Contents

Click any section to jump straight there. This card isn't sticky — scroll back up any time to use it again.

1 What Is CFB Pick'em? 2 Setting Up a Contest 3 My Entries & Making Picks 4 Settings: General 5 Settings: Format & Spreads 6 Settings: Scoring 7 Settings: Picksheet — Pick Rules 8 Settings: Picksheet — Season & Weekly Setup 9 Settings: Segments, Tiebreakers & Groups 10 Settings: Design & Settings Preview 11 Entrants Manager & Invites Manager 12 Leaderboards 13 Auto-Picks & Missed Picks Quick Reference 14 FAQ 15 Glossary
1
Overview

What Is CFB Pick'em?

CFB (NCAAF) Pick'em is a multi-week, private, invite-only contest. Each week, players pick winners — and optionally point totals (over/unders) — for that week's college football slate. It's one flexible product that a commissioner configures to fit their group, running anywhere from Week 1 through the National Championship.

💬
Wording note: Always describe this as an invite-only contest. Avoid saying "free-to-play" — there's no entry-fee mechanic built into the format itself, but "invite-only" is the correct framing CS should use.

The building blocks a commissioner chooses

ChoiceOptionsWhat it means for the group
Contest FormatStandard, or Confidence (Best Pick is a bonus feature layered onto Standard)Standard is straightforward win/loss scoring. Confidence has entrants rank their picks for variable points.
Point Spread ModeStraight Up, Against the Spread (Splash-set), or Against the Spread (Commissioner-set)Straight Up only cares who wins. Against the Spread requires covering a point spread — either Splash's live odds or numbers the commissioner sets by hand.

On top of those choices, commissioners can layer in Segments (mini-leaderboards for a range of weeks), Entrant Groups (team-vs-team standings), tiebreakers, weighted scoring, drop-worst-week, and more — all covered starting in Section 4.

Quick facts CS can lead with

  • Invite-only — there's no entry-fee mechanic built into this format itself.
  • One entrant can hold up to 250 entries per contest if the commissioner raises the cap (default is 1).
  • Supports a full college season: Week 1 through the National Championship, including Bowl Mode for the postseason.
  • Leaderboards exist at three levels — season-long, weekly, and a "picks by week" grid view — plus optional Segment and Group sub-leaderboards.
  • Nearly every commissioner setting can be changed mid-season, with clear rules for what happens to already-graded weeks.
✓

Quick Check

2 questions

1. What's the correct way to describe CFB Pick'em contests?
2. Which two Point Spread Modes let a commissioner (not Splash) set the number entrants have to cover?

2
Getting Started

Setting Up a Contest

Creating a CFB Pick'em contest is a short guided flow: choose the format, name the contest, and land on the commissioner dashboard.

Step 1 — Choose a contest format

Under the Pick'em tab, a commissioner picks from the available CFB formats. This guide covers NCAAF Weekly Pick'em (Straight Up) and its Confidence variant — not the combined NFL + NCAAF formats.

Step 2 — Name the contest

Step 3 — Land on the commissioner dashboard

Once created, the commissioner sees a step-by-step welcome checklist: contest created → set up entry fee collection (Contest Vault, optional) → review contest settings → send invites.

Navigating the contest

The left-hand navigation is the same for every role, but the Commissioner section only appears for the person who created the contest:

  • Entrant nav: My entries, Activity, Contest details
  • Commissioner nav: Dashboard, Contest settings, Entrants manager, Invites manager, Contest Vault
💡
The Dashboard itself doubles as a setup guide — it walks a commissioner through waiving the service fee, reviewing settings, and inviting players, all from one place.
✓

Quick Check

2 questions

1. What's the last step in creating a new contest before landing on the dashboard?
2. Which nav item only appears for the commissioner, not regular entrants?
↑ Back to top
3
Entrant Experience

My Entries & Making Picks

The Entry card

Every entry shows as a card with pick "chips" for each week of the contest — empty and dashed until picks are made.

Click the pencil icon next to the entry name to rename it:

Withdrawing an entry

Click Withdraw in the top-right corner of the entry.

⚠️
Withdrawing is permanent. Once an entry is withdrawn it cannot be added back — the entrant would need to create a brand-new entry (if entries are still open).

Making picks

Clicking Make picks opens the weekly picksheet. The top bar shows which entry is making picks and which week is selected (highlighted in teal). The bottom bar tracks how many picks are still needed for that week.

💡
Always hit "Save picks." Picks are not registered until the entrant clicks Save picks at the bottom of the screen. This is one of the most common reasons a pick "didn't count."

Once picks are saved, the entry card fills in with team logos/abbreviations for each pick made. Click Edit to keep making picks for future weeks — remembering to save every time.

Activity log

The Activity tab is a chronological log of changes to the contest — commissioner settings updates, group membership moves, and pick modifications made on the entrant's behalf. It's split into two tabs: Activity and Pick changes.

✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. An entrant says their picks "disappeared." What's the most likely cause?

4
Contest Settings

General

The General settings page covers the contest name, schedule, and entry controls.

SettingDefaultNotes
Contest Name / DescriptionBlankEditable any time.
Start WeekCFB Week 1 (or current week if created after Week 1)Editable any time. Removing a completed week wipes its picks and emails everyone in the contest.
End WeekConference Championships (extendable to the National Championship)Must be on or after Start Week. Editable any time; removing weeks with picks wipes those picks and emails affected users.
Maximum entries per entrant1Can be raised up to 250, but never lowered once the contest is live.
Entry DeadlineOff. When on: Saturday 12pm ET of the starting week.Editable any time up until the contest's last slate starts.
Lock entries immediatelyOffStops new members from joining right away, regardless of the entry deadline.
Hide Future Picks From CommissionerOffControls whether the commissioner can see/edit a member's future (not-yet-locked) picks.
📌
Joining mid-season: a late joiner is scored 0-for-every-required-pick on all weeks that already completed before they joined. They compete normally starting with the next open week. Joining after the contest has ended is not allowed.
✓

Quick Check

2 questions

1. Can "Maximum entries per entrant" ever be lowered after the contest goes live?
2. A player joins in Week 6 of a CFB Pick'em contest. What happens to Weeks 1–5?
↑ Back to top
5
Contest Settings

Format & Spreads

Scoring mode: Straight Up vs. Against the Spread

ModeHow picks are gradedWho sets the number
Straight UpThe picked team just needs to win outright.N/A — no spread involved (spreads can still be displayed for context only).
Against the Spread — Splash-setThe picked team must cover the spread.Splash's live, dynamically updating odds — these freeze at the spread lock time.
Against the Spread — Commissioner-setSame covering logic.The commissioner manually enters a spread for every game.

Hook vs. Push

Hooked spreads (on by default for Splash-set ATS) add a half-point so a push against the spread can never happen. When spreads aren't hooked, a push is possible, and the commissioner decides how it scores:

  • Count as win — a pushed pick is scored as a win.
  • Count as loss — a pushed pick is scored as a loss.
  • Half point — a pushed pick receives half credit.
📌
Spreads are entered from the home team's perspective: a negative number means the home team is favored, a positive number means the home team is the underdog.
✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. In a Commissioner-set ATS contest, who sets the point spreads?
2. What does "hooking" a spread do?
3. Which of these is NOT one of the three push-scoring options?
↑ Back to top
6
Contest Settings

Scoring

Weighted scoring

Commissioners can apply a per-week point multiplier to amplify certain weeks — for example, giving the National Championship a 4x multiplier so it's worth far more than a regular week. Use 1x for weeks that should keep the default scoring value.

Drop Worst Week

When enabled, each entry's lowest-scoring week (or up to their worst ten weeks) is automatically excluded from their season total — useful for forgiving one bad week. A Last Drop Week setting caps how late in the season a week can still be dropped.

💡
Weighted scoring can even be edited retroactively for weeks that already happened — affected entrants are emailed and scores are automatically recalculated.
✓

Quick Check

2 questions

1. What does setting the National Championship week to "4x" do?
2. What does "Last Drop Week" control?
↑ Back to top
7
Contest Settings

Picksheet — Pick Rules

Pick requirements

Controls when the weekly picksheet locks and how many games players must pick.

SettingDefaultOptions
Pick DeadlineSaturday 12pm ETDefault; first game time; last game time; or a fully custom day/time.
Picks per WeekPick AllPick all games, or any commissioner-typed number up to 100 (a week with fewer games than that number is auto-treated as pick-all).
Best Picks per WeekOffRequires Standard format. Entrants designate one or more "Best Picks" each week for bonus points.
Points per Best PickOff (1, once on)Bonus points awarded for a correct Best Pick.

Autopick

Autopick automatically fills in picks for players who miss the weekly deadline.

  • Who sets it: Commissioner sets one logic for everyone, or each player chooses their own preference when they join.
  • Default team pick logic: All Home Teams, All Away Teams, All Favorites, All Underdogs, or Random.
  • Default Over/Under logic: All Overs, All Unders, or Random.
  • Maximum amount of auto picks: caps how many times a player can be auto-picked over the full contest before missed picks just grade as losses.
⚠️
Autopick can't be combined with a "Must Pick ≥ X games" league minimum — turning one on disables the other.
✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. What's the default CFB-only pick deadline?
2. Which setting must be off before "Must Pick ≥ X games" can be turned on?
3. If a player is capped at 3 max auto-picks and misses a 4th game after hitting that cap, how does it score?

8
Contest Settings

Picksheet — Season & Weekly Setup

Season defaults

Commissioners choose which conferences appear on the picksheet, whether Over/Under totals are enabled, and which days of the week games can appear.

SettingDefaultOptions
Select ConferencesNone (requires AP Top 25 = On)All Conferences, Big Ten, Big 12, ACC, Mountain West, SEC, American Athletic, Conference USA, Mid-American, Sun Belt, Independents.
Include AP Top 25 GamesOnAdds any game involving a Top 25 team, regardless of conference. Top-25 picksheets go live 10am ET every Monday of the CFB season.
Over/Under TotalsOffToggled independently per conference / Top 25.
Eligible Game DaysAll days enabledMulti-select any combination of Mon–Sun.

Weekly customizations

Every week starts by inheriting the season defaults, but a commissioner can override individual weeks: check/uncheck specific games, flag "Must Pick" games, and set per-game point values (1–20).

⚠️
Removing a game (or a conference) that already had picks on it wipes those picks and emails the affected entrants — same for narrowing Eligible Game Days.

Bowl Mode

When enabled, all bowl games combine into a single picksheet and the contest automatically concludes once the CFB season ends (after the National Championship).

⚠️
Bowl Mode is permanent once enabled — it cannot be disabled. It's also not available until later in the season (around bowl selection Sunday in early December), and can be turned on any time up until the National Championship kicks off. Enabling it resets game weights and Must Picks for the bowl slate.
✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. What must be On for a commissioner to include Top 25 games outside their selected conferences?
2. Can Bowl Mode be turned off once it's enabled?
3. A commissioner unchecks a game that already had picks on it for that week. What happens?
↑ Back to top
9
Contest Settings

Segments, Tiebreakers & Entrant Groups

Segments

Segments group weeks into named sub-leaderboards — for example, "First Half" for Weeks 1–9. A commissioner can add any number of segments covering any mix of weeks, and can add or delete them at any time, even retroactively for weeks already played.

Tiebreakers

Commissioners set an ordered cascade of tiebreaker criteria — separately for the weekly leaderboard and the season/segment leaderboard. The first item in the list is the highest priority.

Available tiebreaker add-ons: Tie Game Points, Tie Game Pick, Best Pick Wins, # of Auto Picks Used, Win %, and Win Margin. Points is always active and can never be removed. These are editable at any time, even mid-contest.

Weekly tiebreaker game

Entrants predict the total combined score of a designated game each week — the entrant with the closest guess wins the tiebreaker. Commissioners can select a specific tiebreaker game per week (instead of defaulting to the last game on the picksheet).

Entrant Groups

Groups create team-style sub-leaderboards — useful for office contests where a commissioner wants to pit departments or teams against each other, combining every member's stats into one group score.

Assignment modeWhat it means
Commissioner assigns groupsEntrants join without choosing — the commissioner places them.
Entrants may choose a groupEntrants can optionally select a group when they join.
Entrants must choose a groupEntrants must select an existing group before they can join at all.

An entrant can only belong to one group at a time. At least one group must exist before requiring entrants to choose one.

✓

10
Contest Settings

Design & Settings Preview

Design

Commissioners can upload a custom banner (up to 4 MB, PNG or JPG) as the hero image on the contest page.

Settings Preview

Throughout Contest Settings, a live recap panel sits on the right side of the screen so a commissioner can double-check their configuration at a glance — league, contest type, format, entries, schedule, and scoring, all in one place.

💡
If a member asks "what are the actual rules of my contest?", the Settings Preview panel (or the entrant-facing Rules modal in Section 12/13's Rules link) is the fastest place to verify without digging through every settings tab.
✓

11
Managing Players

Entrants Manager & Invites Manager

Entrants tab

Lists every entrant in the contest with filters for Active, Missing picks, Removed, Paused, and Notes. Click Download in the top-right corner to export the full entrant list.

Email entrants

Lets a commissioner send an email to specific entrants within the contest — useful for reminders about missing picks or upcoming deadlines.

Pick Log

Gives commissioners a log of entrant picks, so they can confirm whether someone actually made picks, when they made them, and whether they edited them afterward. This is one of the best tools for resolving "I definitely made my picks!" disputes — it shows exactly who picked what and when, without exposing anyone's still-live picks to the commissioner.

Invites Manager

Commissioners can build an invite list from several sources — a shareable link, previous Splash entrants, followers, or an imported email list — then send everything as one invite (optionally with a custom message).

💡
The invite link can be copied and pasted anywhere — email, group text, social media — no Splash account required for someone to view it before joining.
✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. An entrant insists they made their picks but the leaderboard shows a zero. Where should CS check first?

12
Standings

Leaderboards

There are three leaderboard views for CFB Pick'em — Season-Long, Weekly, and Picks by Week (grid view) — plus Segment and Group variations layered on top. All views share the same filters: Search Entrants, Following, My Entries, and CSV Export, plus an info icon explaining every column.

Season-Long Leaderboard

ColumnWhat it means
PtsTotal points earned.
W-L-TCumulative wins, losses, and ties across all scored picks this season.
Win%Wins divided by total graded picks.
MarginCumulative margin — margin of victory/defeat (straight up), cover/miss amount (ATS), or +/- the O/U line (totals).
TieDiffTiebreaker differential — absolute difference between predicted and actual score. Lower is better; highlighted green at ≤5.
TG PickWin-loss-tie record specifically on the designated tiebreaker game each week.
BPWBest Pick Wins — number of weeks the entrant's Best Pick was correct.
AutoNumber of weeks with enough auto-picks applied to count as an "autopick week."

Clicking any entrant expands a week-by-week breakdown. A week matching the entrant's dropped week shows an orange background, a strikethrough label, and a DROPPED badge, and is excluded from the season total.

📌
Segment view mirrors the season-long layout, scoped to the weeks in that segment. Group view attributes stats to the group instead of an individual — clicking a group row expands a mini-leaderboard of that group's members, and clicking a member drills further into their week-by-week breakdown. It's a three-level hierarchy: Group → Members → Weeks.

Weekly Leaderboard

ColumnWhat it means
PtsPoints earned this week.
Pot. PtsPotential points — the max this entrant can still finish with, based on picks already made plus pending games.
W-L-TThis week's graded record.
MarginSame margin logic as the season view, scoped to this week.
Win%This week's win rate.
TieDiff (Guess)Differential from the tiebreaker guess (guess shown in parentheses).
TG PickResult on this week's designated tiebreaker game.
BPWWhether this week's Best Pick hit (1) or missed (0).

Expanding a row shows every pick made that week: result, matchup, the pick made (with spread or O/U value if applicable), final score, margin, and points earned.

Badges to know

BadgeMeaning
A / AutoPick was auto-assigned because the entrant missed the deadline.
DroppedThis week was excluded from the season total via Drop Worst Week.
BestThe entrant's designated Best Pick for the week.
TBThe tiebreaker game.
MGRPick was entered by the commissioner on the entrant's behalf (hover shows the timestamp).

Picks by Week (grid view)

  • The entrant column shows the player's name, weekly points, and tiebreaker guess with differential.
  • Every other column is one game — shaded green for correct, red for wrong, yellow for pending, with a colored border only (no shading) while the game is live.
  • Matchups always display as Road @ Home; spreads show from the favored team's perspective; live games show score plus quarter/time remaining; final games show score plus win margin.
✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. What does the "Auto" column track on the season-long leaderboard?
2. On the Picks by Week grid, what color shading means a pick is still pending?
3. What's the hierarchy when drilling into a Group leaderboard?

13
CS Quick Reference

Auto-Picks & Missed Picks

This is one of the most common support topics — "why did I get an auto-pick?" or "why did I score zero?" Use this section as a fast lookup.

  • Auto-Picks are opt-in by the commissioner. If they're off, a missed pick simply scores zero — there is no fallback.
  • When Auto-Picks are on, team picks and Over/Under picks use two separate fallback strategies (e.g., "All Home Teams" for winners, "All Overs" for totals) — set either by the commissioner for everyone, or by each entrant individually.
  • Auto-picks only fill games the entrant hadn't already picked by the deadline — anything already picked is left alone.
  • A commissioner can cap the number of auto-pick events allowed all season (Maximum Auto Picks) and can forgive a small number of missed picks per week before they even count as an auto-pick event (Auto Pick Forgiveness).
  • "Must pick ≥ X games" and Auto Picks are mutually exclusive — turning one on disables the other.

Fast troubleshooting table

Player says…Most likely explanation
"I got auto-picked and I don't know why."They missed the pick deadline for one or more games; Autopick was on for the contest.
"My missed pick scored a zero instead of getting auto-picked."Autopick is off for this contest — no fallback exists, so a miss just scores 0.
"Autopick filled in games I already picked!"This shouldn't happen — autopick only fills games not already picked by the deadline. Check the Pick Log to confirm timing.
"I keep getting auto-picked every week and it's not fair."Check whether Maximum Auto Picks / Auto Pick Forgiveness are configured — or suggest the commissioner enable them.
"Why can't the commissioner turn on Auto Picks?""Must Pick ≥ X games" is likely already on — the two settings can't run at the same time.
💡
The Pick Log (Section 11) is the best tool for confirming exactly when a pick was made or missed — it's the source of truth for these disputes.
✓

Quick Check

3 questions

1. Autopick is OFF for a contest and a player misses a pick. How does it score?
2. Do team picks and Over/Under picks share the same auto-pick fallback logic?
3. A commissioner wants "Must Pick ≥ 3 college games" AND Autopick on at the same time. Is that possible?
↑ Back to top
14
Support

FAQ

Q: Is CFB Pick'em a real-money game?

No — it's a private, invite-only contest. Splash doesn't process entry fees for this format; any money changing hands happens outside Splash unless the commissioner sets up Contest Vault (via LeagueSafe) to collect and distribute payments.

Q: Can a contest run NCAAF Confidence Pick'em instead of Straight Up?

Yes — Confidence is the other Format option. Entrants rank every pick from most to least confident; each correct pick scores its assigned rank value (e.g., a correctly-picked #16 pick earns 16 points, a correctly-picked #1 pick earns just 1). Confidence and Best Pick are mutually exclusive — a contest runs one or the other, never both.

Q: What happens to a missed Confidence pick?

Missed picks consume the entrant's highest (or lowest, commissioner's choice) remaining confidence values first, based on the "Missed Pick Penalty" setting.

Q: Can the Format or League Mode be changed after the contest is created?

No — both are locked once the contest is created.

Q: Can settings be changed mid-season?

Almost all of them, yes — but changes affecting already-completed weeks (like Point Spread Mode, Hooked Spreads, or Weighted Scoring) trigger a re-grade of those weeks and an email to affected entrants.

Q: What's the difference between Segments and Groups?

Segments split the season into sub-leaderboards by time (a range of weeks, like "First Half"). Groups split entrants into sub-leaderboards by team (like departments in an office contest). They can be used together.

Q: What's the Rules link on the picksheet for?

It opens a modal that dynamically explains only the settings actually turned on for that week's contest — game type, Over/Unders, Best Picks, pick requirements, Must Picks, pushes, missed-pick handling, and the tiebreaker. It's one of the best tools for reducing support tickets, since it's always accurate to that specific contest and week.

Q: What are Fast Picks?

One-click shortcuts that auto-fill the whole picksheet: for winners — All Home, All Away, All Favorites, All Underdogs, or Random; for Over/Unders — All Overs, All Unders, or Random. Any Must Pick or league-minimum requirements always take priority over Fast Pick logic.

✓

Quick Check

2 questions

1. Can Confidence and Best Pick both be active in the same contest?
2. What always takes priority over Fast Pick logic?
↑ Back to top
15
Reference

Glossary

TermDefinition
ATSAgainst the Spread — a scoring mode where the picked team must cover a point spread, not just win outright.
AutopickAutomatic fallback picks applied when an entrant misses the deadline; opt-in by the commissioner.
Best PickA Standard-format bonus feature where entrants designate a pick each week for bonus points if correct.
Bowl ModeA postseason setting that combines all bowl games into one picksheet and auto-ends the contest after the National Championship. Permanent once enabled.
ConfidenceA scoring format where entrants rank every pick by confidence for variable point values.
Drop Worst WeekAutomatically excludes an entrant's lowest-scoring week(s) from their season total.
Entrant GroupA team-style sub-leaderboard combining stats from multiple entrants (e.g., office departments).
Hooked SpreadA spread with an added half-point so a push against the spread is impossible.
Must PickA specific game (or minimum number of games) the commissioner requires every entrant to pick.
PushAn exact tie against the spread; scored as a Win, Loss, or Half-Win depending on the commissioner's setting.
SegmentA sub-leaderboard scoped to a custom range of weeks (e.g., "First Half," "Bowl Season").
Straight UpA scoring mode where the picked team just needs to win outright — no spread involved.
TiebreakerA predicted-score guess on a designated game each week, used to break ties in the standings.
Weighted ScoringA per-week point multiplier applied to amplify certain weeks (e.g., 4x for the National Championship).
📌
This guide covers CFB (NCAAF) Pick'em only. NFL-specific defaults, NFL + NCAAF combined contests, and NFL Pick'em settings are documented separately.
